description: Trigger discrimination for the Phase 7 threat & injection cluster: injection defense design vs whole-system threat modeling vs prompt-secrecy vs retrieval authz vs tool scope, and against the shipped security-pr-reviewer and the ai-security-red-team-reviewer subagent.

cases.prompt: Design the layered defense so our agent can't be hijacked by instructions hidden in the documents it reads.
cases.reason: Designing the injection defense-in-depth is this skill's core case; ai-threat-modeler enumerates the risk, rag-security-architect handles retrieval authorization, not the untrusted-content-in-context invariant.

cases.prompt: Give me the full AI risk picture for this agent across all the OWASP LLM categories before we pick which defenses to build.
cases.reason: A cross-category risk enumeration is the threat modeler; this skill builds one named defense once injection is chosen as a risk to close.

cases.prompt: Can an attacker extract our system prompt, and are we wrongly relying on it staying secret?
cases.reason: System-prompt extraction and the prompt-as-control anti-pattern are LLM07 (system-prompt-leakage-reviewer); this skill treats the prompt as non-secret and defends the action boundary.

cases.prompt: Define least-privilege scopes and argument validation for each tool our agent can call.
cases.reason: Per-tool authorization and argument validation is agent-tool-safety-guard; this skill composes that boundary but doesn't design the tool scopes.

cases.prompt: Actually try to jailbreak our deployed assistant and tell me which injection payloads succeed.
cases.reason: Hands-on adversarial probing of a live system is the red-team subagent; this skill designs the defense and the suite, it doesn't execute the attack.
